question: [File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id=eq:X&token.id=gte:Y&token.id=lte:Z combination, causing getLowerFilters() to include all six filter slots (primary.equal, primary.lower, primary.upper, secondary.lower, secondary.equal, secondary.upper) in a single subquery, generating an overly complex WHERE clause that the query planner cannot optimize, degrading performance and preventing token allowance transaction gossip?

[File: 'File Name: rest/controllers/tokenAllowanceController.js -> Scope: High: Preventing gossip of a transaction or multiple transactions'] [Function: extractTokenMultiUnionQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user supply a spender.id=gte:X with a very large X value (near BigInt max), causing the lower subquery to scan zero rows but still execute a full index scan from X to the end of the index, and by sending this at scale, cause repeated wasteful index scans that prevent token allowance transaction gossip?
